workflows.title: Deterministic greedy coloring

stages.title: Maximum-degree coloring target
stages.summary: The target uses Mathlib's graph-coloring API with exactly maximum degree plus one available colors.

stages.title: CT12 vertex-peeling audit
stages.summary: CT12 certifies that the declared finite vertex order is peeled to exhaustion.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult
      (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.problem V) V)
      { context := StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.context object,
        load := object.input.vertices.orderedValues.length,
        state := StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.initialState object.input.vertices.orderedValues }
primaryDeclaration.name: GreedyColoringExample.ct12Run

stages.title: CT4 missing-color steps
stages.summary: Each reverse-fold step uses CT4 functional cardinality to choose a color absent from all later neighbors.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u_1} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (bound : ℕ) →
      (vertex : V) →
        (tail : List V) →
          (tailColoring :
              StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.Partial object (bound + 1)
                (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object tail)) →
            StructuralExhaustion.CT4.ExecutionResult
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepProfile object bound vertex tail tailColoring).spec
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepProfile object bound vertex tail tailColoring).capability
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.context object)
primaryDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepRun

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u_1} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (bound : ℕ) (vertex : V) (tail : List V)
  (tailColoring :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.Partial object (bound + 1)
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object tail)),
  (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.laterNeighbors object vertex tail).length ≤ bound →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepRun object bound vertex tail tailColoring).terminal =
      StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Graph.Terminal.missing
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.step_terminal_missing

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u_1} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (bound : ℕ) (vertex : V) (tail : List V)
  (tailColoring :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.Partial object (bound + 1)
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object tail))
  (headBound : (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.laterNeighbors object vertex tail).length ≤ bound),
  ∀ neighbor ∈ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object tail,
    object.graph.Adj vertex neighbor →
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.selectedColor object bound vertex tail tailColoring headBound ≠
        tailColoring.color neighbor
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.selectedColor_available

evidenceDeclarations.type:
StructuralExhaustion.CT4.ExecutionResult.trace
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepProfile ConcreteK4.object 3 3 [] ConcreteK4.emptyPartial).spec
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepProfile ConcreteK4.object 3 3 [] ConcreteK4.emptyPartial).capability
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.context ConcreteK4.object) ConcreteK4.finalStepExecution =
  [StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Graph.NodeId.assignment,
    StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Graph.NodeId.availability, StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Graph.NodeId.missingTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: GreedyColoringExample.ConcreteK4.ct4_trace_exact

stages.title: Greedy coloring certificate
stages.summary: The reverse fold assembles all CT4 choices into a complete proper Mathlib coloring.

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {left right : ConcreteK4.Vertex},
  ConcreteK4.graph.Adj left right → ConcreteK4.generatedColoring left ≠ ConcreteK4.generatedColoring right
evidenceDeclarations.name: GreedyColoringExample.ConcreteK4.coloring_is_proper

stages.title: CT1 coloring validation
stages.summary: CT1 validates the completed proper coloring against the finite target encoding.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.ct1Encoding V (object.maxDegree + 1)).spec
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.ct1Input object)
primaryDeclaration.name: GreedyColoringExample.ct1Run

stages.title: Maximum-degree coloring theorem
stages.summary: Every explicitly finite simple graph is colorable with one more color than its maximum degree.

primaryDeclaration.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V), Target object
primaryDeclaration.name: GreedyColoringExample.maxDegreeSucc_colorable

workflows.purpose: Expose the CT12 schedule audit, repeated CT4 color selection, constructed coloring, and final CT1 validation.

links.label: audits vertex order
links.description: The finite graph's declared vertex enumeration is the schedule inspected by CT12.

links.label: certifies schedule
links.description: CT12 audits the same vertex order used by the fold; this is orchestration, not a registered residual route.

automationDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u_1} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (bound : ℕ) →
      (values : List V) →
        values.Nodup →
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.BoundedOrder object bound values →
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.Partial object (bound + 1)
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object values)
automationDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.colorOrder

automationDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u_1} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult
      (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.problem V) V)
      { context := StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.context object,
        load := object.input.vertices.orderedValues.length,
        state := StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.initialState object.input.vertices.orderedValues }
automationDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.peelingRun

links.label: repeated reverse fold

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u_1} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (bound : ℕ) →
      (values : List V) →
        values.Nodup →
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.BoundedOrder object bound values →
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.Partial object (bound + 1)
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object values)
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.colorOrder

links.description: Problem-specific recursion repeatedly invokes CT4 and inserts each selected color into the partial coloring.

links.label: validated by CT1
links.description: CT1 checks the proper coloring after it has been constructed by the CT4 fold.

links.label: witnesses colorability
links.description: The complete coloring is the witness required by Mathlib's Colorable predicate.

links.label: target confirmed
links.description: The CT1 success run independently confirms the generated certificate realizes the target.

example.title: Greedy coloring
example.summary: A complete maximum-degree coloring construction combining a CT12 schedule audit, repeated CT4 choices, and CT1 certificate validation.

problemDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u_1} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult
      (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.problem V) V)
      { context := StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.context object,
        load := object.input.vertices.orderedValues.length,
        state := StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.initialState object.input.vertices.orderedValues }
problemDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.peelingRun

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  (capability : StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Capability P) →
    (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Input capability) → StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult capability input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.run

interfaceBindings.description: The graph's ordered vertex list is passed to the reusable list-peeling CT12 profile.

problemDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u_1} →
  (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) →
    (bound : ℕ) →
      V →
        (tail : List V) →
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.Partial object (bound + 1)
              (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.EliminationOrder.verticesFinset object tail) →
            StructuralExhaustion.CT4.FunctionalCardinalityProfile (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.Coloring.problem V)
problemDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.stepProfile

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  (S : StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Spec P) →
    (capability : StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Capability S) →
      (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT4.Input P) → StructuralExhaustion.CT4.ExecutionResult S capability input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT4.run

interfaceBindings.description: The step profile maps used colors to later-neighbor payer slots and supplies CT4's functional witness.

problemDeclaration.type:
(V : Type u) →
  (colorCount : ℕ) → StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetCertificateEncoding fun object => object.graph.Colorable colorCount
problemDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.GreedyColoring.ct1Encoding

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} →
  (S : StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec P) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability S →
      (input : StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Input P) → StructuralExhaustion.CT1.ExecutionResult S input
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.run

interfaceBindings.description: The coloring target encoding exposes the generated coloring and properness proof to CT1.
